description: Context attached to search-related events (e.g. search_suggest_click). Contains the search query text and session information. Use cc_search:searchEntry::TEXT to see what the user typed.

sessionUuid.description: Unique identifier for this search session. A search session starts when the user opens the search box and ends when they close it or navigate away. Use to group all search interactions within one search attempt.

prevSessionUuid.description: UUID of the previous search session, if the user searched multiple times in sequence. NULL if this is the first search session or the previous one is unknown.

searchEntry.description: The current text in the search input at the time of the event. This is what the user typed before clicking on a search suggestion. For example: "stranger th" if they clicked the suggestion for Stranger Things after typing 10 characters.

searchEntries.description: History of search strings from the current session, most recent first. If the user typed "strang", then refined to "stranger things", the array would be ["stranger things", "strang"]. Useful for understanding search refinement behaviour.
